issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query sums all transaction amounts without filtering for completed transactions only.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue

issues.title: Potential performance issue
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to less readable and potentially less performant queries.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose and logic.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t
JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and a readability issue with lack of comments. It also used implicit join syntax which could be improved for performance and readability.
